rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions to avoid '<error>' type diagnostics.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference non-existent or non-visible identifiers.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or types not present in the model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible features and declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type correctness and avoid undefined references.
Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers should be resolved by removing or correcting the clause.
Component type contracts should not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with correct AGREE syntax and types.
Boolean expressions in guarantees must evaluate to type bool.
AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Helper functions and variables must be declared before use in the same annex.
anti_patterns: Do not add new identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to fix unresolved references by inventing new declarations.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component implementations
Model Analyst agent's structural extraction tasks
